description: A designed vertical infographic on a dark navy-blue distressed/grunge textured background listing ICE's 287(g) partnership models. The top header reads, in small caps then large white serif: 'ICE local and state law enforcement PARTNER PROGRAMS'. Down the left edge are three rounded thumbnail photos, each color-tinted to match a section: a red-tinted image of hands gripping prison/cell bars (Jail Enforcement), a blue-tinted image of officers in 'POLICE/SHERIFF' marked gear (Task Force), and a grayscale image of a hand signing a document (Warrant Service Officer). To the right of each is a bold serif heading with description: 'Jail Enforcement Model — ID and process removable aliens you arrest with active or pending criminal charges.' / 'Task Force Model — Allows your officers to enforce limited immigration authority in certain situations.' / 'Warrant Service Officer — Serve and carry out administrative warrants against illegal aliens.' Bottom-left has the DHS seal and 'U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement'; bottom-right the URL 'ICE.gov/287g'. Style is an institutional explainer/recruitment infographic. Messaging technique: clearly marketing the three 287(g) cooperation tiers to police departments, normalizing local-federal immigration enforcement collaboration. Target audience: state and local law enforcement agencies (recruitment of agency partners).
notable_text: ICE local and state law enforcement PARTNER PROGRAMS; Jail Enforcement Model; Task Force Model; Warrant Service Officer; ICE.gov/287g
